commonIssues:
Thousands of e-Khata applications are rejected or pending due to strict digital verification, with many stuck for months due to missing ARO signatures.

How to fix:

  • Ensure all property tax is paid and apply via Seva Sindhu or BBMP e-Aasthi portal. Follow up with the ARO. Seva Sindhu

The system rigidly checks for exact matches between sale deeds, Aadhaar cards, and past tax records. Minor differences cause automatic rejections.

How to fix:

  • Correct your name in Aadhaar or obtain an affidavit for name discrepancy before applying.

Users frequently face issues on the e-Aasthi portal, including session timeouts, OTP delays, and file upload failures.

How to fix:

  • Use the Kaveri Online Services portal during non-peak hours and compress PDFs below 2MB. Kaveri Online Services

A significant hurdle is the lack of mandatory documents like Form 9/Form 11B or the inability to trace the chain of documents (Mother Deed) for older properties.

How to fix:

  • Secure Form 9/11B from Gram Panchayat or apply for a certified copy of the Mother Deed from the SRO.

Owners with B-Khata properties face high difficulty converting to A-Khata/e-Khata, often needing to pay penalties or facing legal ambiguities.

How to fix:

  • Check if the property is eligible for Akrama-Sakrama scheme or pay betterment charges for A-Khata conversion.

Incorrect or missing Property Identification (PID) numbers in old records prevent the new digital system from recognizing the property.

How to fix:

  • Apply for PID correction at the local BBMP ward office with previous tax receipts.

Applications often stay "pending at VAO/RI" or show as "non-transactable" on the Kaveri portal even after submission, halting property sales.

How to fix:

  • Contact the Village Accountant (VAO) or Revenue Inspector (RI) to clear the pending status.

The software often does not indicate why an application was rejected, forcing owners to manually search for issues.

How to fix:

  • Visit the Nadakacheri or Tahsildar office to find the exact cause of rejection.

Even when issued, e-Khatas for jointly owned properties sometimes display only one owner's name instead of all co-owners, causing future ownership disputes.

How to fix:

  • Submit a joint application with all co-owners' Aadhaar to update the e-Khata.

RTCs (Record of Rights, Tenancy and Crops) and mutation reports frequently contain errors, such as wrong area measurements or incorrect owner names.

How to fix:

  • Apply for RTC (Record of Rights) correction via the Bhoomi portal or Nadakacheri. Bhoomi Portal

Discrepancies in old tax payment history often delay the issuance of new documents.

How to fix:

  • Clear all outstanding property tax dues on the BBMP or local municipal portal before proceeding.
